title: Chicken Chaukoni (Nepali)
tagline: A quick onion-forward Nepali semi-dry chicken sabzi that goes perfectly with roti.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients. Light, semi-dry and genuinely high protein — a great sick-day or everyday meal that still tastes proper.
The 100 g dry soya chunks add roughly 25 g protein per serving on top of the chicken for very few calories, and they soak up the masala beautifully in a semi-dry sabzi like this.
Cut the oil from 2 tbsp to 1 tbsp to save ~120 kcal across the pot without hurting the dish.
Skip the soya if you'd rather keep it purely traditional — chicken alone still lands around 30 g protein per serving.

items: 300 g chicken breast (or thigh), cut into medium pieces and marinated
100 g dry soya chunks (50 g per serving)
1 small tomato (or skip and use 3–4 tbsp water)
Optional: garlic, ginger, green chilli, fresh coriander leaves

steps.title: Prep the soya
steps.body: Boil 100 g soya chunks in salted water for 5 min, drain, and squeeze each one dry with your hands — this squeeze matters, or they stay watery and won't take on the masala. Halve any large chunks.

steps.title: Temper
steps.body: Heat 2 tbsp oil in a pan. Add 1/2 tsp fenugreek seeds and let them turn slightly dark (this is the signature chaukoni flavour — don't let them burn black). Add 1/2 tsp cumin seeds.

steps.title: Soften the onions
steps.body: Add 2 sliced onions and sauté just until soft — no need to brown them fully. Add garlic/ginger/green chilli here if using.

steps.title: Sear the chicken
steps.body: Add the 300 g marinated chicken along with salt, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp coriander powder and chilli powder. Spread it out and sear on high for about 2 min until the outside turns white — don't stir too much.

steps.title: Add the soya
steps.body: Stir in the squeezed soya chunks so they start picking up the masala.

steps.title: Cover and cook
steps.body: Add the chopped tomato (or 3–4 tbsp water), stir, cover, and cook on medium 6–10 min until the chicken is cooked through. Breast pieces are usually 90–100% done by the 6-minute mark.

steps.title: Dry it off
steps.body: Open the lid and cook uncovered on medium-high 1–2 min to dry it to a semi-gravy consistency.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Taste for salt. Finish with a pinch of crushed methi seeds or fresh coriander. Serve with roti, paratha or rice.

notes: Doneness check for the chicken: pieces should be opaque all the way through (not glassy), juices clear not pink, texture firm but not chalky.
For juicy breast, don't overcook — sear 2 min, cover 6 min, dry 1–2 min is about 9–10 min total after the chicken hits the pan.
Skipping the tomato makes it even simpler and more onion-forward — just use a few tbsp water to help it cook.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
